task_completion.description: The main website at http://acmecorp.test/ loads successfully without a 503 error.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of a web browser or curl output showing the homepage content of acmecorp.test, indicating the 503 Service Unavailable error (caused by the PHP-FPM socket mismatch) has been resolved.

task_completion.description: The status page at http://acmecorp.test/status.php displays 'System Operational'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of a web browser or curl output for http://acmecorp.test/status.php showing exactly the text 'System Operational'. This proves the database permissions and password mismatch were fixed.

task_completion.description: Internal email delivery is functional and no longer bounces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a successful email delivery log (e.g., /var/log/mail.log showing 'status=sent' to info@acmecorp.test), or the presence of a new email file in /home/acmecorp/homes/info/Maildir/new/, or the Webmin/Usermin interface showing a received test email.

task_completion.description: DKIM signing is enabled for the domain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing successful execution of 'virtualmin set-dkim --enable', or Webmin's DNS/Mail configuration showing DKIM is active, or a DNS query showing the _domainkey TXT record.

task_completion.description: An SPF TXT record is published in DNS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'virtualmin modify-dns --domain acmecorp.test --spf', or a DNS query (e.g., dig TXT acmecorp.test) returning a record starting with 'v=spf1', or Webmin's DNS records page showing the SPF entry.

task_completion.description: A DMARC TXT record is published with a policy of 'reject'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of a DNS query (e.g., dig TXT _dmarc.acmecorp.test) returning 'v=DMARC1; p=reject', or Webmin's DNS records page showing the _dmarc TXT record with the p=reject policy.

integrity.description: The agent verified the website and status page using actual HTTP requests.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a web browser or curl commands targeting http://acmecorp.test. The agent should not just cat or echo the local PHP files to simulate success.

integrity.description: The agent actually sent a test email through the mail system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for commands like 'mail', 'sendmail', or a script sending an email. The agent should not manually create a text file inside the Maildir to fake an email delivery.

integrity.description: The agent actually configured DNS records rather than just echoing the expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with Virtualmin commands (e.g., virtualmin modify-dns), editing of BIND zone files (/var/lib/bind/ or /etc/bind/), or Webmin GUI interactions. Avoid scoring positively if the agent just typed 'echo "v=DMARC1; p=reject"'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target domain is acmecorp.test. The status page is at /status.php and must return exactly 'System Operational'. The DMARC policy must be exactly 'reject' (v=DMARC1; p=reject). The PHP-FPM breakage involves the listen directive being set to 127.0.0.1:9099 instead of the Unix socket. The DB breakage involves db_config.php having root:root ownership (unreadable by web server) and an outdated password ('GymAnything123!' instead of 'MigratedPwd999!'). The Postfix breakage involves a dead relay in /etc/postfix/transport ('smtp:[127.0.0.1]:12345'). Email delivery can be verified by checking /home/acmecorp/homes/info/Maildir/new/.
